ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status For Special Administrator'S Filing Of Accounting For The Period 9/29/17-3/7/18
Off Calendar May-30-2018 Off Calendar

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Apr-03-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Cain for and with Beth Hansen
Amanda Sinclair for and with Leland Small

Mr. Cain states the Court does not have discretion in making the determination of administrator for the estate, citing 8461, and submits on his papers. Ms. Sinclair states there is no ambiguity to 8465 and questions whether Ms. Hansen is petitioning as a private/professional fiduciary. Mr. Cain responds that Ms. Hansen, although not licensed, is a professional fiduciary with over 20 yrs experience. Both Petitions for Letters of Administration are Taken Under Submission. Status for filing of accounting for the period 9/29/17-3/7/18 by Special Administrator is set for May 30, 2018. Status on Close of Estate set for March 13, 2019.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone/Shirley Le. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Apr-03-2018 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Feb-07-2018 Continued To Mar-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-07-2018 Continued To Mar-07-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Thomas Cain, for and with petitioner Beth Hansen; Amanda Sinclair for Leland K. Small.

Ms. Sinclair stated that Beth Hansen is the nominee of issue of the grandparents of the decedent, and that under the provisions of Probate Code section 8465 the appointment of the estate's administrator is up to the Court's discretion rather than determined as a matter of right. Mr. Cain replied that under Probate Code section 8461 the nominee of the great grandparents has priority of appointment. The Court stated that he would like to see supplemental submissions with authorities on the respective positions of counsel.

The following briefing schedule was adopted: Ms. Sinclair will file her objections to Ms. Hansen's petition by February 13, 2018. Mr. Cain will file his response by February 23rd. The Court directed that courtesy copies of the supplemental submissions be provided to the Court. The matter was continued to March 7, 2018 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Ms. Sinclair reported that the special administrator has discovered additional assets of the estate, the bulk of which consists of 10,000 shares in Chevron stock. She requested the granting of IAEA powers. The Court declined to act on that request at this time.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung. (Not reported)
